revision.comment: Created by translating the page "[[:ja:Special:Redirect/revision/81581030|ミヅハノメ]]"
revision.text:
[[File:Creation myths of Japan 4-eng.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''ミヅハノメ'''は、[[Japanese mythology|日本神話]]に登場する[[Kami|神]]である。
== 概要 == 『[[Kojiki|古事記]]』では'''弥都波能売神'''（みづはのめのかみ）、『[[Nihon Shoki|日本書紀]]』では'''罔象女神'''（みつはのめのかみ）と表記する。神社の祭神としては'''水波能売命'''などとも表記される。[[Kuraokami|淤加美神]]とともに、日本における代表的な水の神（[[Suijin|水神]]）である。
『古事記』の[[Kamiumi|神産み]]の段において、[[Kagu-tsuchi|カグツチ]]を生んで陰部を火傷し苦しんでいた[[Izanami|イザナミ]]がした尿から、[[Wakumusubi|和久産巣日神]]（ワクムスビ）とともに生まれたとしている。In the second book of the Nihon Shoki (Chronicles of Japan), it is said that when Izanami was about to die, she gave birth to the goddess Hanyamahime and the goddess of swine, and that Wakumusubi was born between Hanyamahime and Kagutsuchi.
== 解説 == [[Niukawakami Shrine|丹生川上神社]]（[[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]][[Yoshino District, Nara|吉野郡]]）などで淤加美神とともに祀られているほか、各地の神社で配祀神として祀られている。大滝神社（[[Fukui Prefecture|福井県]][[Echizen, Fukui|越前市]]）摂社・岡田神社では、ミヅハノメが村人に紙漉を教えたという伝説が伝わっている。
== 関連項目 ==
* [[List of Japanese deities|日本の神の一覧]] * [[Family tree of Japanese deities|日本の神の家系図]] * [[Kuraokami|淤加美神]] * [[Suijin|水神]] [[Category:Toilet deities]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:Water gods]] [[Category:Japanese gods]]

[[File:Creation myths of Japan 4-eng.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' is a [[Kami|God]] in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].
== Summary == In [[Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami''', and in [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami'''. In the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami'''. [In the [[Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)]], he is called ''Mitsuha-no-me-kami''.
In the [[Kamiumi|Godbirth]] section of the [[Kojiki]], he is said to have been born together with [[Wakumusubi|Wakumusubi]] from the urine of [[Izanami|Izanami]], who was suffering from a burn on her pubic region after giving birth to [[Kagutsuchi|Kagutsuchi]]. In the second book of the Nihon Shoki (Chronicles of Japan), it is said that when Izanami was about to die, she gave birth to the goddess Hanyamahime and the goddess of swine, and that Wakumusubi was born between Hanyamahime and Kagutsuchi.
== 解説 == [[Niukawakami Shrine|丹生川上神社]]（[[Nara Prefecture|奈良県]][[Yoshino District, Nara|吉野郡]]）などで淤加美神とともに祀られているほか、各地の神社で配祀神として祀られている。大滝神社（[[Fukui Prefecture|福井県]][[Echizen, Fukui|越前市]]）摂社・岡田神社では、ミヅハノメが村人に紙漉を教えたという伝説が伝わっている。
== 関連項目 ==
* [[List of Japanese deities|日本の神の一覧]] * [[Family tree of Japanese deities|日本の神の家系図]] * [[Kuraokami|淤加美神]] * [[Suijin|水神]] [[Category:Toilet deities]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:Water gods]] [[Category:Japanese gods]]

[[File:Creation myths of Japan 4-eng.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' is a [[Kami|God]] in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].
== Summary == In [[Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami''', and in [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami'''. In the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami'''. [In the [[Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)]], he is called ''Mitsuha-no-me-kami''.
In the [[Kamiumi|Godbirth]] section of the [[Kojiki]], he is said to have been born together with [[Wakumusubi|Wakumusubi]] from the urine of [[Izanami|Izanami]], who was suffering from a burn on her pubic region after giving birth to [[Kagutsuchi|Kagutsuchi]]. In the second book of the Nihon Shoki (Chronicles of Japan), it is said that when Izanami was about to die, she gave birth to the goddess Hanyamahime and the goddess of swine, and that Wakumusubi was born between Hanyamahime and Kagutsuchi.
== Description == In [[Niukawakami Shrine|Niukawakami Shrine]] ([[Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino-gun]]) and other shrines, he is enshrined together with 淤神神, and is also enshrined as a deity in other shrines throughout Japan. In the Okada Shrine, the main shrine of Otaki Shrine ([[Fukui Prefecture]][[Echizen, Fukui|Echizen City]]), there is a legend that Mizuhanome taught the villagers how to make paper.
== Related items ==
* [[List of Japanese deities|Japanese gods]] * [[Family tree of Japanese deities|Family tree of Japanese deities]] * [[Kuraokami|Yoshikami God]] * [[Suijin|Suijin]] [[Category:Toilet deities]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:Water gods]] [[Category:Japanese gods]]

{{Unreferenced|date=October 2021}}[[File:Creation myths of Japan 4-eng.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' is a [[Kami|God]] in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].
== Summary == In [[Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami''', and in [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami'''. In the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami'''. [In the [[Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)]], he is called ''Mitsuha-no-me-kami''.
In the [[Kamiumi|Godbirth]] section of the [[Kojiki]], he is said to have been born together with [[Wakumusubi|Wakumusubi]] from the urine of [[Izanami|Izanami]], who was suffering from a burn on her pubic region after giving birth to [[Kagutsuchi|Kagutsuchi]]. In the second book of the Nihon Shoki (Chronicles of Japan), it is said that when Izanami was about to die, she gave birth to the goddess Hanyamahime and the goddess of swine, and that Wakumusubi was born between Hanyamahime and Kagutsuchi.
== Description == In [[Niukawakami Shrine|Niukawakami Shrine]] ([[Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino-gun]]) and other shrines, he is enshrined together with 淤神神, and is also enshrined as a deity in other shrines throughout Japan. In the Okada Shrine, the main shrine of Otaki Shrine ([[Fukui Prefecture]][[Echizen, Fukui|Echizen City]]), there is a legend that Mizuhanome taught the villagers how to make paper.

* [[List of Japanese deities|Japanese gods]] * [[Family tree of Japanese deities|Family tree of Japanese deities]] * [[Kuraokami|Yoshikami God]] * [[Suijin|Suijin]] [[Category:Toilet deities]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:Water gods]] [[Category:Japanese gods]]

{{Unreferenced|date=October 2021}}[[File:Creation myths of Japan 4-eng.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref name=":2">{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name=":42">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kami|God]] in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is a [[kami]] of water. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name=":12">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp//eos/detail/|url-status=live|archive-url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref>
Ashiya Shrine is also dedicated to the goddess.<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref>
The [[Niukawakami Shrine]] is dedicated to Mitsuhanome.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Name == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name=":4">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp//eos/detail/|url-status=live|archive-url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name=":4" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name=":1" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== Summary == In [[Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami''', and in [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami'''. In the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami'''. [In the [[Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)]], he is called ''Mitsuha-no-me-kami''.
In the [[Kamiumi|Godbirth]] section of the [[Kojiki]], he is said to have been born together with [[Wakumusubi|Wakumusubi]] from the urine of [[Izanami|Izanami]], who was suffering from a burn on her pubic region after giving birth to [[Kagutsuchi|Kagutsuchi]]. In the second book of the Nihon Shoki (Chronicles of Japan), it is said that when Izanami was about to die, she gave birth to the goddess Hanyamahime and the goddess of swine, and that Wakumusubi was born between Hanyamahime and Kagutsuchi.
== Description == In [[Niukawakami Shrine|Niukawakami Shrine]] ([[Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino-gun]]) and other shrines, he is enshrined together with 淤神神, and is also enshrined as a deity in other shrines throughout Japan. In the Okada Shrine, the main shrine of Otaki Shrine ([[Fukui Prefecture]][[Echizen, Fukui|Echizen City]]), there is a legend that Mizuhanome taught the villagers how to make paper.

== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie [[Your Name]] name is said to come from the goddess.<ref name=":32">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Related items ==
* [[List of Japanese deities|Japanese gods]] * [[Family tree of Japanese deities|Family tree of Japanese deities]] * [[Kuraokami|Yoshikami God]] * [[Suijin|Suijin]]
== References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Toilet deities]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:Water gods]] [[Category:Japanese gods]]

{{Unreferenced|date=October 2021}}[[File:Creation myths of Japan 4-eng.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref name=":2">{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name=":42">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kami|God]] in [[Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. She is a [[kami]] of water. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name=":12">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp//eos/detail/|url-status=live|archive-url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref>
Ashiya Shrine is also dedicated to the goddess.<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref>
The [[Niukawakami Shrine]] is dedicated to Mitsuhanome.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Name == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name=":4">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp//eos/detail/|url-status=live|archive-url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name=":4" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name=":1" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== Summary == In [[Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami''', and in [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami'''. In the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami'''. [In the [[Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters)]], he is called ''Mitsuha-no-me-kami''.
In the [[Kamiumi|Godbirth]] section of the [[Kojiki]], he is said to have been born together with [[Wakumusubi|Wakumusubi]] from the urine of [[Izanami|Izanami]], who was suffering from a burn on her pubic region after giving birth to [[Kagutsuchi|Kagutsuchi]]. In the second book of the Nihon Shoki (Chronicles of Japan), it is said that when Izanami was about to die, she gave birth to the goddess Hanyamahime and the goddess of swine, and that Wakumusubi was born between Hanyamahime and Kagutsuchi.
== Description == In [[Niukawakami Shrine|Niukawakami Shrine]] ([[Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino-gun]]) and other shrines, he is enshrined together with 淤神神, and is also enshrined as a deity in other shrines throughout Japan. In the Okada Shrine, the main shrine of Otaki Shrine ([[Fukui Prefecture]][[Echizen, Fukui|Echizen City]]), there is a legend that Mizuhanome taught the villagers how to make paper.
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie [[Your Name]] is said to come from the goddess.<ref name=":32">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Related items ==
* [[List of Japanese deities|Japanese gods]] * [[Family tree of Japanese deities|Family tree of Japanese deities]] * [[Kuraokami|Yoshikami God]] * [[Suijin|Suijin]]
== References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Toilet deities]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:Water gods]] [[Category:Japanese gods]]

revision.text:
{{Lead too short|date=October 2021}}[[File:Creation myths of Japan 4-eng.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref name=":2">{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name=":4">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kami|God]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. She is a [[kami]] of water. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp//eos/detail/|url-status=live|archive-url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref>
Ashiya Shrine is also dedicated to the goddess.<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref>
The [[Niukawakami Shrine]] is dedicated to Mitsuhanome.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Name == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name=":4"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name=":1"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name=":4" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name=":1" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== Summary == In [[Kojiki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami''', and in [[Nihon Shoki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami'''. In the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami'''. [In the [[Kojiki]], he is called ''Mitsuha-no-me-kami''.
In the [[Kamiumi|Godbirth]] section of the [[Kojiki]], he is said to have been born together with [[Wakumusubi]] from the urine of [[Izanami]], who was suffering from a burn on her pubic region after giving birth to [[Kagutsuchi]]. In the second book of the Nihon Shoki (Chronicles of Japan), it is said that when Izanami was about to die, she gave birth to the goddess Hanyamahime and the goddess of swine, and that Wakumusubi was born between Hanyamahime and Kagutsuchi.
== Description == In [[Niukawakami Shrine]] ([[Nara Prefecture]][[Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino-gun]]) and other shrines, he is enshrined together with 淤神神, and is also enshrined as a deity in other shrines throughout Japan. In the Okada Shrine, the main shrine of Otaki Shrine ([[Fukui Prefecture]][[Echizen, Fukui|Echizen City]]), there is a legend that Mizuhanome taught the villagers how to make paper.
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie [[Your Name]] is said to come from the goddess.<ref name=":3"/>
== Related items ==
* [[List of Japanese deities|Japanese gods]] * [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] * [[Kuraokami|Yoshikami God]] * [[Suijin]]
== References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Toilet deities]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:Water gods]] [[Category:Japanese gods]]

== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref>

The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name=":1" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>

In the [[Kamiumi|Godbirth]] section of the [[Kojiki]], he is said to have been born together with [[Wakumusubi]] from the urine of [[Izanami]], who was suffering from a burn on her pubic region after giving birth to [[Kagutsuchi]]. In the second book of the Nihon Shoki (Chronicles of Japan), it is said that when Izanami was about to die, she gave birth to the goddess Hanyamahime and the goddess of swine, and that Wakumusubi was born between Hanyamahime and Kagutsuchi.
== Description == In [[Niukawakami Shrine]] ([[Nara Prefecture]][[Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino-gun]]) and other shrines, he is enshrined together with 淤神神, and is also enshrined as a deity in other shrines throughout Japan. In the Okada Shrine, the main shrine of Otaki Shrine ([[Fukui Prefecture]][[Echizen, Fukui|Echizen City]]), there is a legend that Mizuhanome taught the villagers how to make paper.
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie [[Your Name]] is said to come from the goddess.<ref name=":3"/>

She is a [[kami]] of water. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url-status=live|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref>
Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name=":4"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name=":1"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name=":4" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name=":1" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== Summary == {{Unreferenced section|date=December 2021}} In [[Kojiki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami''', and in [[Nihon Shoki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami'''. In the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]], she is referred to as '''Mitsuha-no-me-no-kami'''. [In the [[Kojiki]], he is called ''Mitsuha-no-me-kami''.
In the [[Kamiumi|Godbirth]] section of the [[Kojiki]], he is said to have been born together with [[Wakumusubi]] from the urine of [[Izanami]], who was suffering from a burn on her pubic region after giving birth to [[Kagutsuchi]]. In the second book of the Nihon Shoki (Chronicles of Japan), it is said that when Izanami was about to die, she gave birth to the goddess Hanyamahime and the goddess of swine, and that Wakumusubi was born between Hanyamahime and Kagutsuchi.
== Description == {{Unreferenced section|date=December 2021}} In [[Niukawakami Shrine]] ([[Nara Prefecture]][[Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino-gun]]) and other shrines, he is enshrined together with 淤神神, and is also enshrined as a deity in other shrines throughout Japan. In the Okada Shrine, the main shrine of Otaki Shrine ([[Fukui Prefecture]][[Echizen, Fukui|Echizen City]]), there is a legend that Mizuhanome taught the villagers how to make paper.
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie [[Your Name]] is said to come from the goddess.<ref name=":3"/>
== Related items ==
* [[List of Japanese deities|Japanese gods]] * [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] * [[Kuraokami|Yoshikami God]] * [[Suijin]]

{{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} {{Lead too short|date=October 2021}}[[File:Creation myths of Japan 4-eng.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref name=":2">{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name=":4">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[Kami|God]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. She is a [[kami]] of water. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url-status=live|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name=":4"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name=":1"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name=":4" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name=":1" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie [[Your Name]] is said to come from the goddess.<ref name=":3"/>

She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie [[Your Name]] is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>
== Related items ==
* [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] * [[List of Japanese deities|Japanese gods]] * [[Suijin]] * [[Kuraokami|Yoshikami God]]
== See also == * ''[[Id – Invaded|Id:Invaded]]''
== References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Toilet deities]] [[Category:Water gods]] [[Category:Japanese goddesses]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] {{Mizuhanome Faith}}

== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>

{{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>

{{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>
== Related items ==
* [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] * [[List of Japanese deities|Japanese gods]] * [[Suijin]] * [[Kuraokami|Yoshikami God]]
== See also == * ''[[Id – Invaded|Id:Invaded]]''

She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>
== Related items ==
* [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] * [[List of Japanese deities|Japanese gods]] * [[Suijin]] * [[Kuraokami|Yoshikami God]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>
== Related items ==
* [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] * [[List of Japanese deities|Japanese gods]] * [[Suijin]] * [[Kuraokami|Yoshikami God]]
== See also == * ''[[Id – Invaded|Id:Invaded]]''

{{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>
== Related items ==
* [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] * [[List of Japanese deities|Japanese gods]] * [[Suijin]] * [[Kuraokami|Yoshikami God]]

[[category:kk]] [[category:attempting to force efresh]] [[category:1 revision imported]] [[category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Mizuhanome]] {{Mizuhanome Faith}}

{{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>
== Related items ==
* [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] * [[List of Japanese deities|Japanese gods]] * [[Suijin]] * [[Kuraokami|Yoshikami God]]

{{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. {{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>

{{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>

{{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>

{{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>

{{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>

{{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>

{{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>
== Related items ==
* [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] * [[List of Japanese deities|Japanese gods]] * [[Suijin]] * [[Kuraokami|Yoshikami God]]

{{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>

{{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>
== Related items ==
* [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] * [[List of Japanese deities|Japanese gods]] * [[Suijin]] * [[Kuraokami|Yoshikami God]]
== See also == * ''[[Id – Invaded|Id:Invaded]]''

She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>

{{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of [[Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>
== Related items ==
* [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] * [[List of Japanese deities|Japanese gods]] * [[Suijin]] * [[Kuraokami|Yoshikami God]]
