<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Mizuhanome]][[ja:ミヅハノメ]][[pl:Mizuhanome]][[sv:Mizuhanome]][[zh:彌都波能賣神]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Acala|WD=Q337624}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q8191796]] Wikidata name: Mizuhanome [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[:en:Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[:en:Makoto Shinkai|Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[:en:Your Name|Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>

* [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|Japanese gods]] * [[Suijin]] * [[Kuraokami|Yoshikami God]]

She was born from the urine of [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>

== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[:en:Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[:en:Makoto Shinkai|Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[:en:Your Name|Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>

[[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[en:Mizuhanome]]<!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:ミヅハノメ]] [[pl:Mizuhanome]] [[sv:Mizuhanome]] [[zh:彌都波能賣神]] {{wikidata link|Q8191796}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]
{{wikidata link|Q8191796}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Mizuhanome]][[ja:ミヅハノメ]][[pl:Mizuhanome]][[sv:Mizuhanome]][[zh:彌都波能賣神]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Acala|WD=Q337624}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q8191796]] Wikidata name: Mizuhanome [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[:en:Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[:en:Makoto Shinkai|Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[:en:Your Name|Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>

* [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|Japanese gods]] * [[Suijin]] * [[Kuraokami|Yoshikami God]]

She was born from the urine of [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>

== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[:en:Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[:en:Makoto Shinkai|Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[:en:Your Name|Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>
== See Also ==
* [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|Japanese gods]] * [[Suijin]] * [[Kuraokami|Yoshikami God]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Mizuhanome]][[ja:ミヅハノメ]][[pl:Mizuhanome]][[sv:Mizuhanome]][[zh:彌都波能賣神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Mizuhanome]][[ja:ミヅハノメ]][[pl:Mizuhanome]][[sv:Mizuhanome]][[zh:彌都波能賣神]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Acala|WD=Q337624}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q8191796]] Wikidata name: Mizuhanome [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[:en:Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[:en:Makoto Shinkai|Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[:en:Your Name|Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>
== See Also ==
* [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|Japanese gods]] * [[Suijin]] * [[Kuraokami|Yoshikami God]]

She was born from the urine of [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[:en:Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[:en:Makoto Shinkai|Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[:en:Your Name|Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>

She was born from the urine of [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]].<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>

== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In [[:en:Romanization of Japanese|romaji]] her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director [[:en:Makoto Shinkai|Makoto Shinkai]] the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''[[:en:Your Name|Your Name]]'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>

== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director {{ill|Makoto Shinkai|en|Makoto Shinkai|WD=Q335080|lt=Makoto Shinkai}} the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''{{ill|Your Name|en|Your Name|WD=Q21697406|lt=Your Name}}'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>
== See Also ==

She was born from the urine of {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}}.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Mizuhanome]][[ja:ミヅハノメ]][[pl:Mizuhanome]][[sv:Mizuhanome]][[zh:彌都波能賣神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Mizuhanome]][[ja:ミヅハノメ]][[pl:Mizuhanome]][[sv:Mizuhanome]][[zh:彌都波能賣神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Mizuhanome]][[ja:ミヅハノメ]][[pl:Mizuhanome]][[sv:Mizuhanome]][[zh:彌都波能賣神]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Acala|WD=Q337624|1=Acala}}}} {{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}}.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In {{ill|Romanization of Japanese|en|Romanization of Japanese|WD=Q192509|lt=romaji|1=Romanization of Japanese}} her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director {{ill|Makoto Shinkai|en|Makoto Shinkai|WD=Q335080|lt=Makoto Shinkai|1=Makoto Shinkai}} the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''{{ill|Your Name|en|Your Name|WD=Q21697406|lt=Your Name|1=Your Name}}'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>
== See Also ==
* [[Family tree of Japanese deities]] * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=Japanese gods|1=List of Japanese deities}} * [[Suijin]] * [[Kuraokami|Yoshikami God]]
== See also == * ''{{ill|Id – Invaded|en|Id – Invaded|WD=Q77844091|lt=Id:Invaded|1=Id – Invaded}}''
== References == {{Reflist}}
{{Izanami-firebirths}}
{{Mizuhanome Faith}}

{{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Acala|qid=Q337624|1=Acala}}}} {{Short description|God in Japanese mythology}} [[File:Mizuhanome ai art.webp|thumb]] [[File:Descendants of Izanami alone-en.svg|thumb|468x468px|Gods born from Izanami's illness and death (based on the Kojiki) Displayed in SVG (supported browsers only)]] '''Mizuhanome''' (彌都波能売神<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mizuhanome • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mizuhanome/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> or みつはのめのかみ<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a">{{Cite web|title=弥都波能売神 – 國學院大學 古事記学センターウェブサイト|url=http://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/mitsuhanomenokami/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>) is a [[kami|god]] of water in [[Japanese mythology]]. She was born from the urine of {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}}.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9361|access-date=2021-09-16|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>
== Worship == She is enshrined at [[Atago Shrine (Tokyo)|Atago Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=愛宕神社について {{!}} 愛宕神社|url=https://www.atago-jinja.com/about/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=www.atago-jinja.com}}</ref> Ashiya Shrine<ref>{{Cite web|title=彌都波能売神|url=https://www.ashiyajinja.or.jp/saisin/saisin-30/|access-date=2021-09-16|website=芦屋神社-公式サイト-|language=ja}}</ref> and [[Niukawakami Shrine]] are also dedicated to her.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a">{{Cite web|title=映画『君の名は。』のヒロインのモチーフ。雨乞い・雨止めの神様とは？（ダイヤモンド・オンライン）|url=https://news.yahoo.co.jp/articles/01dc04bf1e84c04f0edd1f5b7d51eef4a2d41ab7|access-date=2021-09-16|website=Yahoo!ニュース|language=ja}}</ref>
== Names == She is also referred to as '''Mizuhanome'''<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a"/> or '''Itsu no Mitsuhanome no kami'''.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a"/> In {{ill|Romanization of Japanese|en|Romanization of Japanese|qid=Q192509|lt=romaji|1=Romanization of Japanese}} her name is read as '''Mitsuhanomenokami'''.<ref name="kojikikokugakuinacjp-2021a" />
The ''mitsu'' or ''mizu'' in her name means water and ''me'' is a feminine suffix.<ref name="國學院大學テシタルミューシアム-2021a" /> Her name is explained as ''water snake woman'' or ''water dragon woman''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Eder|first=Matthias|date=1957|title=Review of The Mother-Son Complex in Japanese Religion and Folklore|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1177369|journal=Folklore Studies|volume=16|pages=297–299|doi=10.2307/1177369|issn=0388-0370|jstor=1177369}}</ref>
== In popular culture == {{Main|Shinto in popular culture}} According to director {{ill|Makoto Shinkai|en|Makoto Shinkai|qid=Q335080|lt=Makoto Shinkai|1=Makoto Shinkai}} the name for the character Mitsuha Miyamizu from the movie ''{{ill|Your Name|en|Your Name|qid=Q21697406|lt=Your Name|1=Your Name}}'' is said to come from the goddess.<ref name="yahooニュース-2021a"/>
